title: Campomanesia sellowiana
taxonomy: Campomanesia sellowiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is native to South America and is commonly known as guanabara or guanabra. The plant belongs to the genus Campomanesia, which includes several other species of myrtle trees. Campomanesia sellowiana is classified as a medium-sized tree, growing up to 10 meters tall, with a broad, rounded crown. Its leaves are elliptical in shape and have a leathery texture.
uses: Campomanesia sellowiana, also known as guanabira or guava de monte, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's leaves and fruits are used to make infusions, which are believed to help with digestive issues and as a remedy for fever. In some parts of South America, the plant's roots are used to treat various ailments, including rheumatism and skin conditions. The plant's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties are also being studied for their potential applications in modern medicine. Further research is needed to confirm the medicinal uses and effectiveness of Campomanesia sellowiana.
